title: Specialization · NLP / NLU (deep dive)
focus: Tokenization, encoder vs decoder vs encoder-decoder, NER, classification, summarization.

items.label: BERT (encoder) — masked LM
interviewQuestions: How does BERT's masked-LM pretraining differ from GPT's causal LM?
When would you use BERT for retrieval vs classification?
Why is BERT no longer state-of-the-art for many NLP tasks?

items.label: T5 (encoder-decoder) — text-to-text
interviewQuestions: Why is T5's text-to-text framing useful?
When would you choose an encoder-decoder over a decoder-only model?
How does T5 handle multi-task training?

items.label: GPT family (decoder) — autoregressive
interviewQuestions: Why has the decoder-only architecture won for general LLMs?
How does in-context learning emerge from autoregressive training?
What's the trade-off vs encoder-decoder for tasks like translation?

items.label: Named entity recognition (NER) tagging schemes (BIO, BILOU)
interviewQuestions: Compare BIO vs BILOU tagging — what do you gain with BILOU?
How would you handle nested or overlapping entities?
Why is span-based NER often better than token-level for modern models?

items.label: Abstractive vs extractive summarization
interviewQuestions: Compare abstractive vs extractive summarization — when each is the right call.
How would you evaluate an abstractive summarizer without a gold reference?
What's the failure mode when the model invents facts in summarization?

items.label: Design a multilingual customer support classifier
Pick an evaluation suite for your NLP system (BLEU / ROUGE / BERTScore / human)

interviewQuestions: Encoder vs decoder vs encoder-decoder — when do you pick each?
How do transformers handle different modalities (text → image → audio)?
Walk me through how you'd evaluate a summarization system.
Why is tokenization the most common cause of multilingual NLP bugs?
